one_time_description: Large share of income from Government Business Enterprise (Nigig Power Corporation) of $31,506,537 due to equity pickup from wind energy partnership; also includes $2,196,681 promissory note expense
economic_notes: First Nation owns Nigig Power Corporation (NPC), a government business enterprise involved in wind energy (Henvey Inlet Wind LP). Also has interests in Shwe Miikaan Limited Partnership (construction, 33.33%) and HIW Property Holdings LP (50%). Operates a gas bar and trailer park. Pickerel Contracting Limited is a consolidated subsidiary. FNFA loan of $107.5M taken out in 2021 related to wind energy financing.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021). Large FNFA loan of $107.5M taken out during year to on-lend to Nigig Power Corporation via note receivable ($117M issued, $10.5M repaid). Massive swing in GBE equity pickup: +$31.5M in 2021 vs -$23.9M in 2020, driving the $33.2M surplus vs -$21.7M prior year loss. Legal contingency: First Nation is defendant in $173.2M breach of contract claim jointly with NPC. Two consolidated subsidiaries: Henvey Inlet First Nation Key Harbour Settlement Trust and Pickerel Contracting Limited. One GBE: Nigig Power Corporation. Two non-controlled entities accounted for by modified equity: SMLP and HIWP. No remuneration schedule included in the extracted text. total_government_transfers estimated as federal funding $3,597,227 + provincial funding $1,283,410 + Kinoomaadziwin Education Body $1,298,964 = $6,179,601 (rounded to $6,179,437 per line items). OSR includes Henvey Inlet Wind LP, grants and other subsidies, and Other revenue lines.
